title: Avoiding pg_dump --inserts

performance_problem.metric_impact: Track primary_io_wait_ms: primary read/write latency unchanged while backup runs from a replica.
performance_problem.business_impact: If Avoiding pg_dump --inserts regresses, users see slower completion or retries; track support contacts, abandonment, and the technical metric together.

reasoning: perf-249 treats Avoiding pg_dump --inserts as a database bottleneck. The fix is valid only when EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS), pg_stat_statements, lock/connection wait telemetry, and a rollback rehearsal show primary_io_wait_ms (milliseconds) meeting the row target: primary IO wait unchanged while backup runs off-primary; correctness output must remain unchanged and the rollback path must be explicit.
solution:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
: "${PG_REPLICA_URL:?replica required}"
pg_dump "$PG_REPLICA_URL" --format=custom --no-owner --no-acl --file=./backup.dump
pg_restore --list ./backup.dump >/tmp/backup.manifest

# Avoid --inserts/--column-inserts for bulk backup restore paths; COPY/custom format is smaller and faster unless a downstream SQL-only tool explicitly requires INSERTs.
before_metrics: perf-249 baseline for Avoiding pg_dump --inserts: record primary_io_wait_ms in milliseconds, sample at least five cold and five warm runs, keep the production-shaped fixture stable, and capture the user-visible symptom before changing code.
after_metrics: perf-249 optimized result for Avoiding pg_dump --inserts: primary_io_wait_ms must meet target (primary IO wait unchanged while backup runs off-primary), with no correctness diff, no adjacent-metric regression, and artifacts attached to the CI or review record.
trade_offs: Avoiding pg_dump --inserts may add index/storage/operational complexity; keep the old path or rollback migration until EXPLAIN and production metrics prove the database budget holds.
regression_prevention: CI stores an EXPLAIN/pg_stat snapshot for Avoiding pg_dump --inserts; fail if primary_io_wait_ms regresses, row scans/locks increase, or the rollback migration is missing.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive fix for Avoiding pg_dump --inserts changes the nearest slow line without measuring primary_io_wait_ms. It can move cost into another database boundary, hide cold-start or tail behavior, and leave the original user-visible symptom outside automated budgets.
